epics.name: Core Game Engine
epics.description: Snake movement, collision detection, food consumption mechanics

epics.name: Game State & Scoring
epics.description: Score tracking, speed management, game lifecycle

epics.name: Game UI Controls
epics.description: Buttons and menus for game flow (new, pause, resume, stop, replay list)

epics.name: State Recording & Persistence
epics.description: Serialize game states for replay, persist completed games to storage

epics.name: Replay Playback
epics.description: Display replay list, playback at variable speeds, bit-exact accuracy verification

epics.name: Ghost Snake Overlay
epics.description: Render ghost snake during replay, simultaneous live game and replay without interference

epics.name: Input Handling
epics.description: Arrow key buffering, prevent reverse direction in single move

epics.name: Canvas Rendering
epics.description: Draw grid, snake, food, ghost snake on HTML5 Canvas

stories.title: Snake moves in response to arrow key input
stories.i_want: to press arrow keys to control the snake's movement direction
stories.so_that: I can navigate the snake toward food and avoid collisions
acceptance_criteria: Snake moves up when Up arrow is pressed and held
Snake moves down when Down arrow is pressed and held
Snake moves left when Left arrow is pressed and held
Snake moves right when Right arrow is pressed and held
Direction change takes effect on the next game tick (not immediately)
Multiple direction presses are queued, only latest valid direction executed per tick
test_cases: T: Press Up → snake moves up on next tick
T: Press Right → snake moves right on next tick
T: Press Down, then Up → snake moves down (buffer respects order)
T: Press Right, then Left within same tick → snake does not reverse (see S-7.2)

stories.title: Snake grows when it consumes food
stories.i_want: the snake to grow by one segment when it eats food
stories.so_that: my snake becomes longer and the game becomes harder
acceptance_criteria: When snake head touches food, food is consumed
Snake length increases by 1
New body segment appears at previous head position
Tail does not move (snake extends, not just translates)
test_cases: T: Snake length 3, eat food → length becomes 4
T: Head at food position → food removed, new segment at prev head
T: Multiple food consumptions → snake length increases each time

stories.title: Food spawns at random empty grid cells
stories.i_want: food to appear at random locations on the grid
stories.so_that: the game remains unpredictable and engaging
acceptance_criteria: Food spawns at game start at random empty cell
After food is consumed, new food spawns at different random empty cell
Food never spawns on any segment of the snake
Food never spawns outside grid boundaries
Food position is random (not biased to edges or center)
test_cases: T: Game start → food appears on grid
T: Eat food → new food appears at different location
T: Run 10 games → food positions vary
T: Large snake → food spawns on empty cell, never on snake

stories.title: Collision detection ends game
stories.i_want: the game to end when the snake hits a wall or itself
stories.so_that: I understand when I've failed and can try again
acceptance_criteria: Game ends when snake head moves beyond grid boundary
Game ends when snake head occupies same cell as any body segment
Game-over state prevents further input and movement
Score and final length are displayed
test_cases: T: Snake at right edge → move right → collision, game ends
T: Snake coiled → move into self → collision, game ends
T: Post-collision → arrow key pressed → no effect
T: Game-over → final score displayed

stories.title: Score increments when food is consumed
stories.i_want: to see my score increase by 1 each time I eat food
stories.so_that: I can track my progress and compare with previous games
acceptance_criteria: Score starts at 0
Score increments by 1 after each food consumption
Score is displayed on screen during active game
Score is preserved in replay metadata
test_cases: T: Game start → score = 0
T: Eat food → score = 1
T: Eat 5 foods → score = 5
T: Game over → final score matches total foods eaten

stories.title: Speed increases as snake grows
stories.i_want: the game to get faster as my snake grows longer
stories.so_that: the challenge scales with my progress
acceptance_criteria: Initial game speed is 5 FPS (200ms per tick)
Speed increases by 1 FPS for each food item consumed
Speed does not exceed 15 FPS (physical cap for playability)
Speed change is reflected in game tick rate on next food consumption
test_cases: T: Game start → FPS = 5
T: Eat 1 food → FPS = 6
T: Eat 10 foods → FPS = 15 (capped)
T: Tick timing confirms FPS change

stories.title: Track game lifecycle (start, play, pause, end)
stories.i_want: the game state to transition correctly through play/pause/end
stories.so_that: I have full control over the game flow
acceptance_criteria: Game transitions from Menu → Playing when 'New Game' clicked
Game transitions from Playing → Paused when 'Pause' clicked
Game transitions from Paused → Playing when 'Resume' clicked
Game transitions from any state → Menu when 'Stop' clicked
Game automatically transitions to ended state on collision
test_cases: T: Menu → New Game → Playing state
T: Playing → Pause → Paused state, grid frozen
T: Paused → Resume → Playing state continues
T: Playing → Stop → Menu state
T: Collision → Ended state, score saved

stories.title: New Game button initializes fresh game
stories.i_want: to start a fresh game by clicking 'New Game'
stories.so_that: I can play multiple rounds
acceptance_criteria: Button click starts new game with snake at center, 3 segments
Food spawns at random empty cell
Score resets to 0
Speed resets to 5 FPS
Generation counter resets to 0
Game immediately begins ticking
test_cases: T: Click New Game → game starts, snake visible at center
T: Previous game score = 10 → New Game → score = 0
T: New Game → button label changes to 'Pause'

stories.title: Pause and Resume buttons control game tick
stories.i_want: to pause the game, then resume it from where I paused
stories.so_that: I can take a break without losing progress
acceptance_criteria: Pause button stops game ticking and input processing
Grid state is preserved exactly
Pause button label changes to 'Resume'
Resume button continues from paused state without loss
Resume button label changes back to 'Pause'
test_cases: T: Pause → grid frozen, tick stopped
T: Pause → Resume → game continues from exact state
T: Pause, move snake → no effect until Resume

stories.title: Stop button returns to main menu
stories.i_want: to stop the current game and return to the menu
stories.so_that: I can play a different game or view replays
acceptance_criteria: Stop button halts game or replay immediately
Game state is cleared from canvas
Main menu is displayed with 'New Game' and 'View Replays' buttons
Game in progress is NOT recorded to replay list if stopped mid-play
test_cases: T: Playing → Stop → menu appears
T: Paused → Stop → menu appears
T: Replay playing → Stop → menu appears, replay ended

stories.title: View Replays button displays replay list
stories.i_want: to see a list of my completed games
stories.so_that: I can select one to watch as a replay
acceptance_criteria: Clicking 'View Replays' shows list of completed games
Each replay entry shows score, timestamp, and duration
List is sorted by timestamp (newest first)
Empty message shown if no replays exist
Each replay has a delete option
test_cases: T: No games completed → 'No replays available'
T: 3 games played → list shows 3 entries with scores
T: Replay list sorted newest first

stories.title: Delete button removes replay from list and storage
stories.i_want: to delete old replays to manage storage space
stories.so_that: I can keep only my best games
acceptance_criteria: Delete button appears next to each replay in list
Clicking delete shows confirmation dialog
Confirming deletion removes replay from list and localStorage
Cancelled deletion leaves replay intact
test_cases: T: List has replay → click delete → confirm → replay gone
T: Delete → cancel → replay still in list

stories.title: Serialize complete game state each tick
stories.i_want: to capture every game state during play
stories.so_that: accurate replays can be generated
acceptance_criteria: Every game tick is recorded with snake position, food position, score, generation
Recording includes player input sequence for deterministic replay
Serialization format is compact (< 100KB for 100-tick game)
No data loss during recording
test_cases: T: Play 50 ticks → 50 states recorded
T: Each state includes snake, food, score
T: Replay all states → produces original game

stories.title: Save completed game to replay list and localStorage
stories.i_want: to persist completed games for future replay
stories.so_that: players can watch replays across sessions
acceptance_criteria: When game ends, final recording is saved to localStorage
Recording includes metadata: score, timestamp, duration
Replay metadata appears in replay list immediately
localStorage persists across browser session restarts
test_cases: T: Game ends → replay appears in list
T: Browser restart → replay list preserved
T: Multiple games → all appear in list

stories.title: Handle localStorage quota exceeded gracefully
stories.i_want: to know when storage is full and manage space
stories.so_that: I don't lose game data due to quota limits
acceptance_criteria: When localStorage quota exceeded, warning is displayed
Player is prompted to delete old replays
Delete option is offered to free space
test_cases: T: Many games saved → storage full → warning appears
T: Delete replay → space freed, new recording succeeds

stories.title: Select a replay from list and begin playback
stories.i_want: to click a replay and watch it play automatically
stories.so_that: I can review my previous games
acceptance_criteria: Clicking a replay in list starts automatic playback
Playback begins at 1× speed (normal game speed)
Ghost snake animates through all recorded states
Speed controls are visible during playback
test_cases: T: Select replay → playback starts immediately
T: Replay progresses frame by frame
T: Speed controls visible and responsive

stories.title: Playback speed controls (0.5×, 1×, 2×, 4×)
stories.i_want: to adjust replay playback speed
stories.so_that: I can watch in slow motion or fast forward
acceptance_criteria: Speed control buttons visible during replay: 0.5×, 1×, 2×, 4×
Clicking a speed multiplier updates playback immediately
0.5× plays at half speed (slower for analysis)
4× plays at 4× speed (faster overview)
Current speed is visually highlighted
test_cases: T: Replay at 1× → click 2× → playback speeds up
T: Replay at 2× → click 0.5× → playback slows down
T: Speed button shows current selection

stories.title: Pause and resume replay playback
stories.i_want: to pause a replay and resume it later
stories.so_that: I can analyze specific frames
acceptance_criteria: Pause button stops replay animation
Grid frozen on current replay frame
Resume button continues from paused frame
Manual frame stepping possible while paused
test_cases: T: Replay playing → Pause → animation stops
T: Replay paused → Resume → continues from same frame
T: Paused replay → speed change → works on resume

stories.title: Verify bit-exact replay accuracy
stories.i_want: to ensure replayed games produce identical state sequences
stories.so_that: replays are trustworthy and deterministic
acceptance_criteria: Replayed game produces same snake positions frame-by-frame as original
Food positions match exactly at each tick
Scores match exactly at each tick
No variation in replay across multiple playbacks
test_cases: T: Record game → replay twice → both identical
T: Compare replay frame N with original frame N → match
T: Verify scores and positions bit-exact across replays

stories.title: Render ghost snake distinct from live snake
stories.i_want: to visually distinguish the ghost snake from the live snake
stories.so_that: I can follow both game states simultaneously
acceptance_criteria: Ghost snake appears in faded color (lower opacity)
Live snake appears in solid, vibrant color
Both snakes are clearly visible on same canvas
No visual artifacts or overlap confusion
test_cases: T: Replay running → ghost snake rendered in faded color
T: Live game overlapped with ghost → both visible, distinct colors
T: No rendering glitches or performance degradation

stories.title: Start new live game while replay is running
stories.i_want: to play a new game while watching a ghost replay simultaneously
stories.so_that: I can compare my live strategy with a previous game
acceptance_criteria: New Game button available during active replay
Clicking New Game starts live game without stopping replay
Both live snake and ghost snake render on same canvas
Live game and replay tick independently
test_cases: T: Replay at 1× → click New Game → live game starts
T: Live and ghost both visible, moving independently
T: Input affects only live snake, not ghost

stories.title: Ensure no collision logic between live and ghost snakes
stories.i_want: the ghost snake to not interfere with my live game outcome
stories.so_that: I can focus on my own gameplay without ghost interference
acceptance_criteria: Ghost snake occupies same cell as live snake without collision
Live snake collision detection only checks live snake body
Food consumption only affects live snake, never ghost
Live game ends only on live snake collision, not ghost collision
test_cases: T: Live and ghost occupy same cell → no collision
T: Live snake eats food while ghost is on same cell → only live grows
T: Live snake dies → game ends, ghost continues

stories.title: Buffer arrow key input for smooth play
stories.i_want: my rapid key presses to be queued and processed in order
stories.so_that: I don't lose inputs during fast gameplay
acceptance_criteria: Arrow keys are buffered in a queue
Queue is processed one direction per game tick
Only the latest direction in queue is executed per tick
Old directions are discarded if newer ones arrive
test_cases: T: Press Up, Down rapidly → Down is executed (latest)
T: Press Left, Up, Right → Right is executed
T: Input queue processes one per tick

stories.title: Prevent snake from reversing direction in single move
stories.i_want: the snake to not reverse into itself due to rapid inputs
stories.so_that: I don't accidentally kill myself with a quick key press
acceptance_criteria: Pressing Right then Left rapidly does not cause reverse
Only the current valid direction is executed
Invalid reverse direction is ignored or buffered for next tick
Snake never moves into its own body as a result of input buffering
test_cases: T: Snake moving Right → press Left → snake continues Right
T: Snake moving Right → press Left before next tick → no reverse collision
T: Rapid Right-Left-Right sequence → no self-collision
T: Correctness bar: rapid input never causes invalid move

stories.title: Draw grid on HTML5 Canvas
stories.i_want: to render the 30×30 game grid on canvas
stories.so_that: players can see the game state
acceptance_criteria: Canvas displays 30×30 grid cells
Grid cells are clearly visible and sized consistently
Grid background is light color for contrast
Grid dimensions match requirements (30×30)
test_cases: T: Canvas loaded → grid visible with correct dimensions
T: Grid cells are square and evenly spaced
T: Canvas responsive to window size

stories.title: Draw live snake and food on canvas
stories.i_want: to render the live snake segments and food item
stories.so_that: players see the current game state
acceptance_criteria: Snake head rendered in solid green
Snake body segments rendered in slightly lighter green
Food rendered in red/yellow contrasting color
Updates on each game tick without lag
test_cases: T: New game → snake visible at center, food elsewhere
T: Move snake → segments follow head
T: Each tick → snake and food redrawn correctly

stories.title: Draw ghost snake in faded style on canvas
stories.i_want: to render ghost snake with lower opacity and optional dash pattern
stories.so_that: ghost is visually distinct from live snake
acceptance_criteria: Ghost snake rendered with ~50% opacity
Ghost snake color similar to live but noticeably faded
Optional: ghost segments drawn with dashed or dotted outline
Ghost rendering does not cause performance lag
test_cases: T: Replay running → ghost snake rendered in faded style
T: Live + ghost on canvas → both visible, distinguished
T: No performance degradation with dual rendering

ambiguities: Canvas cell size not specified; assumed 10-15px cells for 30×30 grid visibility
Ghost snake style (dashed vs. opacity) left to implementation; opacity assumed primary distinction
Food color not specified; assumed red or yellow for contrast with green snake
